Renard's Belt of Quickness  
 

Lore Item No Trade
Slot: WAIST
AC: 6
STR: +5 DEX: +12 CHA: +5 AGI: +12
Haste: +41%
WT: 1.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ROG
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Renard's Belt of Quickness
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: WAIST
AC: 6
STR: +5 DEX: +12 CHA: +5 AGI: +12
Haste: +41%
WT: 1.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ROG
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Armor
Stackable:No
Lucy Entry By:Enduraence
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-14 17:45:25
Page Updated:Sat Jul 25th, 2020

Expansion: Original
Rarity: Ultra Rare
Level to Attain: 46
[Comments ]

This item is the result of a quest.
Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
Rogue Test of Trickery

Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Plane of Sky


Uploaded August 31st, 2019 by iventheassassin
Updated September 1st, 2019
Send a correction
Post Comment
equipping haste weapons/armor
QuoteReply
# Jul 24 2005 at 4:24 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591839254938
Scholar
163 posts
i have a type 4 aug on RB that procs(i forget the name) peotection 3 or sumthing which reduces some of the incoming melee damage. if im being hit or was taking some damage, i will switch out my ragebringer with my lifetap proccing weapons and with my 36% haste cloak i wont lose much haste. once my hp bar is back up(which also saves mana for the healers)i will switch back to faster more damaging weapons. this is what makes the difference in having haste effect weapons and haste effect armor. and the bandolier with the DoN expansion makes this very simple
▲ Page top
Confused?
QuoteReply
# Jul 20 2003 at 5:27 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592257828077
12 posts
Okay real fast, for you those of you that are slightly confused, or those that just want some more defined information on haste, haste %'s, item's with haste, stacking infromation, and haste spells and songs go to this website. It is by far the most comprhensive site on the subject that I have ever seen. It also includes information on how to find your new delay of a weapon after being hasted

Here's That Link:
http://cloud.prohosting.com/~talone/HasteData.htm

Edited, Sun Jul 20 18:19:50 2003
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: May 14 2002 at 1:14 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Worn or not the effect of ragebringer is not in the same catagory as this haste, it counts as a bard haste type. Thus is stacks, i have both Ragebringer and CoF, i have also done the test to confirm haste statistic and the haste total was correct at 76%. The ragebringer will stack with this haste item as well.
RE: Seething fury is a bard type of haste
QuoteReply
# Jun 10 2002 at 6:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966237371
1 post
HAHAHAHAHA, Seething Fury is NOT a bard song type effect.

It is permanant and worn. So your RB and CoF will not stack together. Stop smoking the crack.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Mar 20 2002 at 4:46 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) The haste on the RB is a spell proc that is in effect as long as it is equiped, this belt WILL STACK with the ragebringer.
Do you even have a Ragebringer?!
QuoteReply
# Apr 04 2002 at 8:45 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1618187462655
25 posts
The haste on RB (Seething Fury) is not a proc... it is a worn effect, just like this belt. The effects do *NOT* stack. In order for them to stack, the ragebringer would have to create a buff icon in your buff box via a proc (Through use.. as in you swing til it goes off).

Johsia Destruere
56th Rogue
▲ Page top
Haste %
QuoteReply
# Feb 18 2002 at 11:31 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1594570642552
6 posts
Ragebringer is 41% Haste

I understand this belt is 36% or 41%. I would tend to think it is 36% haste. Haste is perm effect and if you have multiple haste items , the item with the most haste will prevail. Perm Haste items will only stack with Haste Spell and Songs, no two alike will stack.
You can also not compare this item even if it is 41% to the Ragebringer for one reason....Ragebringer is a weapon and this is an item. One will not be desired more or less due to haste only. If I make sense to you...good. If not...listen better. )

Dapippo the Rogue
59 Rogue Horde of Shadows
The 7th Hammer
▲ Page top
RE: Haste %
QuoteReply
# Jul 19 2002 at 5:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591768398088
9 posts
Ragebringer is 40% , listen to 1,000's of rogs on safehouse.

Haste Stacking
QuoteReply
# Jun 04 2001 at 1:15 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591758551190
4 posts
Whichever item has the better haste is the one you'll get. I was under the impression that the Ragebringer was the best rogue equipable haste item in the game, but am not absolutely certain of that.

▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Feb 14 2001 at 11:43 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Haste is supposedly 50%
RE: Haste %
QuoteReply
# Feb 23 2001 at 5:30 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592247500208
12 posts
What exactly is meant by Haste in EQ terms though?
▲ Page top
RE: Haste %
QuoteReply
# Apr 12 2001 at 7:50 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591758234951
Scholar
26 posts
The haste percentage is the additional number of attacks you get when you wear the item in a given ammount of time. So if you look at a weapon with 20 delay, it attacks every 2 seconds, or 30 times a minute. 50% haste would increase that ammount by 50%, giving you 45 attacks per minute (essentially making the delay of the weapon 13.3 delay instead of 20 delay).
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 24 2001 at 4:51 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) wouldnt it make it 10dly? seems 20*.5 is 10 for me, not 13.3.....

#Anonymous, Posted: Feb 07 2001 at 2:30 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) i like to blow elephants
